commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P2] The PR is draft, protected by the maintainer label, merge-conflicting, and has both a concrete validator regression and a hosted-feed trust-boundary decision that need maintainer/author refresh.; Needs attention: The diff has a concrete trust-boundary concern because it removes freshness validator checks before serving persisted remote marketplace metadata.; Review finding: [P1] Preserve snapshot validator ch... (sha=13b94e75dbbb74e2868620679730f90098cab63e)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds a SQLite-backed hosted external plugin catalog snapshot store, makes hosted catalog loading default to shared-state snapshots, updates state schema/types, and adds persistence tests.

PR surface: Source +699, Tests +542, Generated +26, Other +5. Total +1272 across 7 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the patch regression: current main has validator checks and tests for hosted snapshots, while the PR diff removes both. I did not run tests because this review is read-only, but the source diff is enough to reproduce the failure path.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The PR is draft, protected by the maintainer label, merge-conflicting, and has both a concrete validator regression and a hosted-feed trust-boundary decision that need maintainer/author refresh.

target.title: Persist hosted catalog snapshots in state
target.body:
<!-- hosted-feed-stack:start -->
## Hosted Marketplace Feed Stack

This is PR4 in the hosted marketplace feed stack. The stack should land in order, with maintainer review at each step before the later PRs wire hosted marketplace data into broader user flows.

1. #95846: refactor the bundled external plugin catalog toward the feed-shaped data model.
2. #95868: add the lazy hosted marketplace feed loader for the configured public feed URL, with guarded HTTPS fetch, checksum, schema, size, timeout, and bundled fallback behavior.
3. #95877: add accepted hosted snapshot fallback so a failed or unchanged hosted fetch can reuse the last valid feed payload.
4. #95964: persist hosted marketplace feed snapshots in OpenClaw state.
5. #95969: add marketplace source profile validation for hosted feed install candidates.
6. #95981: add configurable `marketplaces.feeds` and `marketplaces.sources` profiles.
7. #96155: add `openclaw plugins marketplace refresh` as the explicit manual snapshot refresh path.
8. #96158: add `openclaw plugins marketplace entries` as the explicit manual read path over hosted, snapshot, or bundled fallback entries.
9. #96194: add marketplace feed telemetry.

The broader follow-up after this stack is to decide whether and how marketplace entries should feed install/search/startup behavior, add automatic conditional refresh using `ETag` / `Last-Modified`, or move to ClawHub producer-side validation for `/v1/feeds/plugins` and `/v1/feeds/skills`.
<!-- hosted-feed-stack:end -->

## Stack Context

This is step 4 in the hosted feed rollout, stacked on `feeds-hosted-catalog-snapshot-cache` after:

- #95846 refactors the bundled external plugin catalog into a feed-shaped document.
- #95868 adds the hosted feed loader with guarded HTTP fetch, validation, checksums, and conditional request metadata.
- #95877 adds the in-process snapshot fallback contract for 304 and transient hosted feed failures.

## RFC Alignment

This PR is the persistent snapshot plumbing for the hosted catalog loader. It implements the SQLite storage part of RFC #19 rollout item 6: store the latest accepted hosted feed snapshot in OpenClaw state so the client can keep using a last-known-good catalog during HTTP 304 responses or transient hosted-feed failures.

It intentionally does not claim the full lifecycle refresh implementation. Named feed/source profiles, source-ref validation, signed envelopes, scheduled refresh, `maxStale`, and feed freshness reporting are still follow-up PRs.

## Summary

- Adds a SQLite-backed snapshot store for hosted official external plugin catalog responses in OpenClaw state.
- Makes the hosted feed loader use that state-backed store by default while preserving `snapshotStore: null` for callers and tests that need persistence disabled.
- Persists the feed body plus URL, status, ETag, Last-Modified, locally computed checksum, save time, and update time.
- Lets HTTP 304 and transient hosted feed failures use the last known good state snapshot before falling back to the bundled catalog.
- Keeps snapshots keyed by the resolved feed URL, which leaves room for later named feed/source profile work without mixing feeds.

## Deliberately Not Included

- Named local feed/source profile configuration.
- `sourceRef` validation against configured source profiles.
- Authenticated feed URLs or secret-reference resolution.
- Signed feed envelopes or trust-key configuration.
- Background refresh scheduling, `maxStale`, or stale-catalog status reporting.
- Search, install UX, skills, or telemetry.

Those remain follow-up steps after the core fallback storage path is reviewable.

## Validation

- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.ts src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.test.ts src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog-snapshot-store.ts src/state/openclaw-state-schema.generated.ts src/state/openclaw-state-db.generated.d.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxlint --deny-warnings src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.ts src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.test.ts src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog-snapshot-store.ts src/state/openclaw-state-schema.generated.ts`
- `pnpm db:kysely:check`
- `git diff --check`
- `CI=1 timeout 180s node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.test.ts`
- `pnpm tsgo:core:test`
- `codex review -c model=gpt-5.5 --base origin/feeds-hosted-catalog-snapshot-cache`

## Real Behavior Proof

Behavior or issue addressed:
This PR persists accepted hosted marketplace feed snapshots in OpenClaw SQLite state so later marketplace commands can reuse a last-known-good payload.

Real environment tested:
WSL Ubuntu 24.04, `/root/src/openclaw-feeds-hosted-catalog-prep`, cumulative hosted marketplace feed stack through PR8 at `13cea8e00e7`, built source tree with `pnpm build`, isolated OpenClaw state via `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-feeds-proof.rKXnUX`, and the live ClawHub feed endpoint `https://clawhub.ai/v1/feeds/plugins`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

1. `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.test.ts src/cli/plugins-cli.marketplace-refresh.test.ts src/cli/plugins-cli.marketplace-entries.test.ts src/plugins/marketplace.test.ts src/config/zod-schema.marketplaces.test.ts --reporter=dot`
2. `pnpm build`
3. `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-feeds-proof.rKXnUX node openclaw.mjs plugins marketplace entries --feed-url https://clawhub.ai/v1/feeds/plugins`
4. `find /tmp/openclaw-feeds-proof.rKXnUX -maxdepth 3 -type f | sort`

Evidence after fix:
Focused tests passed across 3 Vitest shards: `1 passed / 8 tests`, `2 passed / 7 tests`, and `2 passed / 70 tests`. `pnpm build` completed successfully. The live CLI entries command returned `Source: hosted`, `Entries: 59`, `Feed: clawhub-official sequence 9`. The isolated state directory contained `/tmp/openclaw-feeds-proof.rKXnUX/state/openclaw.sqlite` after the marketplace command path ran.

Observed result after fix:
The cumulative stack can use a clean OpenClaw state directory, create SQLite state, and list live hosted marketplace entries.

What was not tested:
This proof did not directly inspect SQLite rows. Snapshot write/read behavior and schema shape are covered by focused tests.
target.author: giodl73-repo

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
